key_items: Bitcoin most oversold since 2020 crash with RSI mirrors suggesting 50-30% rebound potential to $70K
Bitcoin risks new 'purge' with bear-market losses still $35B below 2022 total indicating bottom may not be in
Bitcoin Has Dumped All Gains Since Trump Reelection - down 50% from peak; $2.6B short trap brewing but offset by macro headwinds
reasoning: Bitcoin faces severe short-term headwinds from macroeconomic factors (strong jobs data pushing rate expectations higher, $900B Treasury cash rebuild draining liquidity, AI capital rotation) and technical breakdown below $60K, though extreme oversold conditions (RSI echoing 2020/Feb 2026) and massive short positioning create meaningful rebound potential within the 1-7 day swing window. The contradictory signals suggest a volatile, choppy range with upside risk to $65-70K but downside risk to $55K, making this an unfavorable risk-reward for new entries or aggressive positioning.
risks: Continued macro deterioration (Fed rate expectations, liquidity drain from Treasury rebuilding, AI capital competing with crypto allocation) could drive BTC below $55K support, triggering cascading liquidations and invalidating oversold rebound thesis.
